region_of_origin: Global (widely consumed across Asia, Middle East, and beyond)
food_subgroup: Hot and cold brewed beverages — plain tea (unsweetened)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of brewed plain tea (approximately 240 ml / 240 g), served hot or cold without sugar or milk.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, brewed, prepared with tap water
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service — USDA National Nutrient Database for Standard Reference Legacy Release (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for plain brewed tea prepared with tap water (FDC ID 171917, formerly NDB No. 14355). Values represent unsweetened black tea infusion per 100 g (approximately 100 ml). USDA SR Legacy is an approved secondary global source under Project Veritas v2 source policy. This entry is the canonical reference for plain brewed tea and is appropriate for the GrabFood listing of Tea served at Sahara Bar and Restaurant and Lucky Cafe in Singapore.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal, American Diabetes Association)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain unsweetened brewed tea contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g per USDA SR Legacy (FDC ID 171917). Because it contains no glycemic carbohydrates, it has no measurable glycemic index. Per Project Veritas v2 zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) and gi_category is set to low. Glycemic load per 240 ml serving = (1 x 0.0) / 100 = 0.0. The Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables (Diabetes Care, peer-reviewed) confirm that non-carbohydrate beverages such as plain tea carry no glycemic load. This is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database treatment of non-carbohydrate beverages.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (3 mg per 100 g); no added salt
hypertension.guidance: Plain unsweetened tea is appropriate for individuals with hypertension; its sodium content is negligible at 3 mg per 100 g, and regular consumption of black or green tea has been associated with modest reductions in systolic blood pressure in meta-analyses of randomised controlled trials.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates; no impact on postprandial blood glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain unsweetened tea contains no carbohydrates and has a glycemic load of zero per serving, making it a safe and recommended beverage choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; polyphenols in tea may additionally support insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and antioxidant polyphenol content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain unsweetened tea contains no saturated fat or cholesterol and provides flavonoids and catechins that have been associated with reduced LDL oxidation and improved endothelial function in cardiovascular disease prevention research.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Plain tea (brewed, unsweetened) is one of the world's most widely consumed beverages, prepared by steeping dried tea leaves (Camellia sinensis) in hot water. It is served across virtually every culture and cuisine, including Middle Eastern, Southeast Asian, South Asian, East Asian, and Western contexts. In Singapore and Malaysia, plain unsweetened tea is commonly called Teh Kosong or Teh O Kosong (Malay/Hokkien), meaning tea without milk and without sugar. In the Middle Eastern context, it is often served as a hot black tea. Nutritionally, plain brewed tea is essentially calorie-free, containing approximately 1 kcal per 100 g, with no protein, no fat, and no carbohydrates. Its sodium content is negligible at 3 mg per 100 g. Tea is rich in polyphenolic antioxidants including catechins, theaflavins, and thearubigins, which have been studied for their roles in cardiovascular protection, anti-inflammatory activity, and potential support of insulin sensitivity. Because it contains no glycemic carbohydrates, plain tea has a glycemic load of zero and is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. It is naturally vegan, halal, kosher, gluten-free, and dairy-free. The GrabFood Singapore listing for this item appears at Sahara Bar and Restaurant (Boat Quay) and Lucky Cafe (North Canal Road), priced between SGD 1.40 and SGD 4.36, likely reflecting variations in serving size, type of tea, or restaurant positioning.

Multilingual names are verified standard terms.
notes: 1. energy_kcal is 1.0 kcal per 100 g (trace caloric content from minor dissolved compounds in the infusion); this is not zero but effectively negligible. 2. protein_g is naturally zero — plain brewed tea contains no protein by nature; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171917. 3. fat_g is naturally zero — plain brewed tea contains no fat by nature; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171917. 4. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero — plain brewed tea contains no glycemic carbohydrates by nature; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171917. 5. sugar_g is naturally zero — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 6. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 7. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Project Veritas v2 zero-calorie beverage glycemic convention. glycemic_load_per_serving = 0.0 as carbohydrate_g = 0. 8. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are 0.0 — naturally zero for plain brewed tea; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 9. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero for a plant-based infusion. 10. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg are 0.0 — confirmed zero or below detection threshold per USDA SR Legacy; these are trace or absent in plain brewed black tea. 11. calcium_mg is 0.0 per USDA SR Legacy for plain brewed tea. 12. Input record described this as Indonesian tea with a hint of sweetness but the GrabFood listing name is simply Tea and the restaurants (Sahara Bar and Restaurant, Lucky Cafe) are not specifically Indonesian. The description has been corrected to plain brewed unsweetened tea as the canonical form; sweetened variants would require a separate record. 13. Input cuisine tag was Middle Eastern based on Sahara Bar and Restaurant; however, plain tea is a globally universal beverage and has been classified accordingly. 14. potassium_mg (37.0), iron_mg (0.02), vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (0.03), phosphorus_mg (1.0), magnesium_mg (3.0), zinc_mg (0.02) are small but non-zero values confirmed per USDA SR Legacy. 15. Nutrient source is USDA SR Legacy — an approved secondary global source under Project Veritas v2 source policy (Section 5.1, item 5: USDA Foundation Foods / SR Legacy for raw ingredients, staples, and generic foods). GI source is Atkinson et al. 2008 published in Diabetes Care, a peer-reviewed journal, which is an approved GI-specific source under Project Veritas v2 source policy (Section 5.1, item 8).
